what_it_does: Acceptance script for the structural half of the convergence sign-off. It builds a live node registry, calls coherence_detect.structural_signoff on the repository tree, and asserts that the verdict has the expected shape, fails loudly when live node types are missing, and currently passes.
observations: Defines a small check(label, cond) helper that asserts, increments a global PASS counter, and prints ok.
Discovers node types from ROOT/nodes and feeds live_types into structural_signoff.
Calls structural_signoff twice: once with live_types and once without, expecting the latter to return pass=False.
Expects the verdict to contain pass, reasons, and evidence keys, with evidence containing new_orphans and registry_drift.
Expects all reasons to be concrete strings, not vague opinions.
The current integrated tree is expected to pass the structural sign-off.
standouts: Fail-loud design: missing live_types makes the registry check fail rather than silently pass.
Treats the verdict as a hard boolean gate that can block a convergence merge.
Explicitly pairs this structural half with a separate by-use operator path (guided-review).
Uses adversarial language like 'can't be green-painted' and 'honesty-instrument' in the docstring.
conventions: Docstring states the file's role in a larger convergence process.
Uses a global mutable counter for passed checks.
Uses plain assert instead of a test framework.
Imports from sibling packages under ROOT.
concerns: Global PASS counter and side effects inside check() make the script order-dependent.
Temporary store directory is created but not explicitly cleaned up.
No test framework harness; assertions stop the script on first failure, so later checks may be masked.
Calls structural_signoff twice, which may duplicate expensive tree-wide work.
notes: A single-file acceptance gate that exercises the structural convergence sign-off API and insists on a fail-loud, boolean, merge-blocking verdict.
questions: What exact checks does structural_signoff perform under new_orphans and registry_drift?
What is the guided-review by-use operator path and how is it invoked?
What does 'convergence merge' refer to in this repository's workflow?
How are node types registered and what qualifies as a live type?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to serve as coherence's structural half of the convergence sign-off, and the file does exactly that by running structural_signoff and asserting the expected outcomes.
intention_signals: To make the structural acceptance of a convergence round an objective, automated, non-negotiable gate that cannot be satisfied by superficially positive signals, while leaving human judgment to a paired by-use review path.
novelty: The file frames the test as an 'honesty-instrument' that is 'can't-be-green-painted' and explicitly positions itself as one half of a two-part sign-off, contrasting exact structural checks with operator-guided review.
contribution: A runnable acceptance contract for the structural sign-off API: it records the expected verdict shape, the fail-loud behavior when live node types are absent, and the expectation that the current integrated tree passes.
summary_for_embedding: An acceptance test that invokes coherence_detect.structural_signoff over the whole repository tree and asserts a boolean, merge-blocking structural verdict with no new orphans and no registry drift.
intention_for_embedding: To codify the structural half of the convergence sign-off as an exact, fail-loud, automated gate that complements a separate operator-driven review path.

symbols.does: Asserts a labeled condition, increments the global PASS counter, and prints an ok message.
symbols.description: A lightweight acceptance helper that combines enforcement (assertion) with bookkeeping (global PASS tally) and progress output, used to verify each structural sign-off expectation in sequence.
